Petition To Approve Compromise Of Pending Action Person With A Disability
Off Calendar Jun-12-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Pending Action of an Adult with a Disability.
Dept. 514. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Petitioner Giorgio Frabboni appeared via telephone as Guardian ad Litem for Enrica Chili, who was not present at this morning's hearing.
Petitioner was placed under oath and testified.
Court Certified Italian speaking interpreter Deborah Schowalter assisted Mr. Frabboni at this morning's hearing.
Aimee G. Hamoy, Esq. appeared on behalf of defendants Board of Trustees of the California State University and Peter Biella.
Order appointing Mr. Frabboni Guardian ad Litem for Enrica Chili was filed with the Clerk's Office on February 26, 2016.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and the compromise is approved as follows:

Settlement Amount - $3,000,000.00.
Attorney Fees - $1,200,000.00.
Costs - $168,159.21.

The net balance of $1,631,159.79 shall be used to establish a Qualified Settlement Fund ("QSF") within the meaning of Section 468B of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986. Terms & conditions of the QSF are set forth in Attachment 7(c)(2)(b) of the Order Approving the Compromise of a Pending Action of an Adult with a Disability.

Order Approving the Compromise of a Pending Action of an Adult with a Disability Disputed was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Matter is CONTINUED to August 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for Progress Report on the establishment & funding of the Qualified Settlement Fund.

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS, BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE and PETER BIELLA'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED in its entirety. Liberally construed, the second amended complaint alleges that Mr. Meigaro was an employee of CSU and therefore his acts or omissions could be attributable to CSU under Vehicle Code 17001. The internal guidelines alleged in paragraphs 62 and 63 are relevant to the issue of duty and breach for the fourth cause of action. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS, BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE and PETER BIELLA'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Defendant CSUs demurrer to the third cause of action for negligent hiring, retention and supervision of CSU employees is sustained with twenty days leave to amend and defendants CSU and Peter Biella's demurrer to the fourth cause of action for negligent supervision of plaintiff is overruled. Public entities are not directly liable under Government Code Section 815.2. Plaintiff has not alleged that CSU is vicariously liable for its employee's negligent hiring, training, or supervision of Mr. Biella and Mr. Meigaro and is given leave to correct this deficiency if she can do so in good faith. Plaintiff has adequately alleged that defendants had sufficient control and regulation over plaintiff to support the fourth cause of action. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
